source.title: States versus Rewards: Dissociable Neural Prediction Error Signals Underlying Model-Based and Model-Free Reinforcement Learning

confidence_rationale: Demonstrates anatomically and computationally dissociable prediction error channels: a state prediction error (SPE) in intraparietal sulcus and lateral PFC (model-based, prospective/simulational) and a reward prediction error (RPE) in ventral striatum (model-free, realized outcome). This two-channel architecture directly parallels REE's pre-commit simulation channel vs post-commit realized-error channel. The write-boundary enforcement claim requires the signals to stay architecturally separated; this paper shows the brain maintains that separation neuroanatomically. Confidence moderate rather than high because the paper does not address 'write-boundary enforcement' or contamination consequences if channels are blended -- it establishes dissociability but not the pathological consequences of mixing.
